items.title: Somalia: A Report Warns Somalia Security Deteriorating Amid Political Divisions
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- The European Union's asylum agency has warned that Somalia's security situation has significantly worsened, citing deepening political divisions and growing instability that have allowed the Al-Shabaab militant group to expand its influence.
items.titleIt: Somalia: A Report Warns Somalia Security Deteriorating Amid Political Divisions

items.title: Ambassador Tommasi Leaves Sudan: ‘Compromises Necessary for Lasting Peace’
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 04 LUG - Italy’s Ambassador to Sudan, Michele Tommasi, carried out his final mission in Port Sudan at the end of his four-year term. “I take with me a deep admiration for the dignity and resilience of the women and men of this country ,” he said during a farewell reception, “as well as the confidence that they will find the strength and wisdom necessary to reach the compromises essential for building lasting peace and a future of stability and prosperity.” The visit also provided a further opportunity to strengthen the bilateral partnership, with a particular focus on the he
items.titleIt: Ambassador Tommasi Leaves Sudan: ‘Compromises Necessary for Lasting Peace’

items.title: African Union peacekeeping mission in Somalia at risk amid US funding cuts
items.description: Washington said its decision was due the government’s lack of progress in containing the al-Shabaab group or in taking ownership of its own security.
items.titleIt: African Union peacekeeping mission in Somalia at risk amid US funding cuts

items.title: Humanitarian Corridors: 27 Refugees from Ethiopia Arrive in Italy
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 03 LUG - The process that enabled 27 Somali and Sudanese refugees from Ethiopia to depart for Italy—carried out through the “humanitarian corridors” program—has been successfully completed. The initiative, the result of a memorandum of understanding between the Community of Sant’Egidio, the Ministries of the Interior and of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation, and the Italian Episcopal Conference, represents a significant example of institutional cooperation and international solidarity. The embassy, which has been collaborating with the Community of Sant’Egidio on
items.titleIt: Humanitarian Corridors: 27 Refugees from Ethiopia Arrive in Italy

items.title: US to block UN funding for Somalia in blow to AU peace mission
items.description: In a diplomatic note to the African Union seen by Reuters, the US has vowed to block any spending of UN funds on the AU Support ​and Stabilisation Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM).
items.titleIt: US to block UN funding for Somalia in blow to AU peace mission

items.title: Somalia Honors Italy for Health Cooperation
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 03 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Somalia, Pier Mario Daccò Coppi, received an honorary award from the management of the Forlanini Hospital in Mogadishu—which was recently modernized thanks to funding from Italian Cooperation—at the conclusion of a meeting dedicated to healthcare cooperation between the two countries. During the meeting, the hospital’s general director, Mohamed Jafar Ali, outlined the progress made by the facility, focusing on the healthcare services offered, ongoing development projects, and the creation of new wards, particularly those dedicated to women’s
items.titleIt: Somalia Honors Italy for Health Cooperation

items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Kenya: Police Tighten Nairobi Security With Checkpoints Ahead of Defiant Saba Saba Protests
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Police will mount enhanced checkpoints across Nairobi on Tuesday in a major security operation ahead of planned Saba Saba demonstrations, even as activists insist they will proceed with the protests despite warnings that any gathering will be treated as unlawful.
items.titleIt: Kenya: Police Tighten Nairobi Security With Checkpoints Ahead of Defiant Saba Saba Protests

items.title: OPEC+ boosts oil production as Gulf exports recover
items.description: Seven OPEC+ producers have agreed to increase oil production from August, signalling confidence that Gulf exports are recovering after months of disruption caused by the Middle East conflict. The move comes as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz gradually returns to normal.
items.titleIt: OPEC+ boosts oil production as Gulf exports recover

items.title: Woman describes 'ordeal' after ECHR reverses Italy 'sexist verdict'
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 6 - A woman who was awarded damages after the European Court of Human Rights quashed what it called a "sexist and stereotypical" verdict" acquitting her sexually abusive husband because "it's normal for a man to overcome a woman's resistance" before sex told an Italian daily Monday that she had been through an "ordeal" since she reported the domestic rape in 2021. "Justice has finally been done. It doesn't seem real after so long. I went through an ordeal," Audrey Ubeda, now 42, said in an interview with la Repubblica, after the ECHR ruled in her favour, ordering Italy to co
items.titleIt: Woman describes 'ordeal' after ECHR reverses Italy 'sexist verdict'

items.title: Egypt makes history, heartbreak for Cape Verde at World Cup
items.description: Heartbreak for Cape Verde as the World Cup debutants bowed out after a thrilling 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina. The Blue Sharks twice battled back to level the match, pushing Lionel Messi's side to the limit in one of the tournament's biggest surprise performances.
